citations.narrative:
Two recently purchased dogs were housed in the garage in plastic transportation crates. One Dachshund measured 19"
(+6=25 inches, requiring 625 square inches of space). The crate measured 18 inches X 23 inches =414 square inches.
The other Dachshund measured 21 inches (+6 = 27 inches, requiring 729 square inches). The crate measured 22 X 32
inches=704 square inches. Dogs that are not provided with the minimum space requirements cannot make normal
postural movements, nor have enough distance from where they urinate & defecate, sleep, eat and drink.
Each dog housed in a primary enclosure (including weaned puppies) must be provided a minimum amount of floor space,
calculated as follows: Find the mathematical square of the sum of the length of the dog in inches (measured from the tip
of its nose to the base of its tail) plus 6 inches; then divide the product by 144. The calculation is: (length of dog in inches
+ 6) x (length of dog in inches + 6) = required floor space in square inches. Required floor space in inches/144 = required
floor space in square feet.
The dogs must be provided with their minimum space requirements at all times.
Corrected during inspection on 11 February 2026
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with a facility representative.
n

narrative:
3.6(c)(1)(i) 
   
Primary enclosures. 
Two recently purchased dogs were housed in the garage in plastic transportation crates. One Dachshund measured 19" 
(+6=25 inches, requiring 625 square inches of space). The crate measured 18 inches X 23 inches =414 square inches.  
The other Dachshund measured 21 inches (+6 = 27 inches, requiring 729 square inches). The crate measured 22 X 32 
inches=704 square inches. Dogs that are not provided with the minimum space requirements cannot make normal 
postural movements, nor have enough distance from where they urinate & defecate, sleep, eat and drink.  
 Each dog housed in a primary enclosure (including weaned puppies) must be provided a minimum amount of floor space, 
calculated as follows: Find the mathematical square of the sum of the length of the dog in inches (measured from the tip 
of its nose to the base of its tail) plus 6 inches; then divide the product by 144. The calculation is: (length of dog in inches 
+ 6) x (length of dog in inches + 6) = required floor space in square inches. Required floor space in inches/144 = required 
floor space in square feet. 
The dogs must be provided with their minimum space requirements at all times. 
 
Corrected during inspection on 11 February 2026 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with a facility representative.
